cell_type_sentences: Genome-wide association meta-analysis identifies 17 loci associated with nonalcoholic fatty liver disease.
Nonalcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD) is common and partially heritable and has no effective treatments.
Individuals in the top 10% and 1% of genetic risk have a 2.5-fold to 6-fold increased risk of NAFLD, cirrhosis and hepatocellular carcinoma.
